refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability ≥ 0.999 (99.9%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the bidirectional command-and-telemetry link is operational to total evaluation time.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-010 addresses a critical operational requirement: remote equipment control depends on reliable communication. The refined FR quantifies the acceptance criterion (≥0.999 availability), specifies the measurement scope (bidirectional link), and defines the evaluation window. This is essential for safety-critical teleoperation where link loss can lead to equipment immobilization or unsafe states.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous monitoring of link state (up/down) during the evaluation period; calculation of availability as (total_up_time / total_evaluation_time); comparison against 0.999 threshold. Measurement shall include all causes of unavailability: network outages, equipment downtime, handover delays.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-010.1: The teleoperation system shall detect link loss within [X] milliseconds.
FR-NGHE-010.2: The teleoperation system shall initiate safe-state transition upon link loss.
FR-NGHE-010.3: The teleoperation system shall support redundant communication paths to achieve target availability.
FR-NGHE-010.4: The teleoperation system shall log link state transitions for availability audit.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Single communication link with best-effort reliability (no redundancy)
DP-B: Dual independent communication paths (primary + secondary) with automatic failover
DP-C: Multi-carrier network aggregation with load balancing and seamless handover
DP-D: Hybrid approach: primary cellular + secondary satellite with intelligent switching
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Dual independent communication paths (primary + secondary) with automatic failover and link-state monitoring

potential_sources: DP-B couples to FR-NGHE-010.2 (safe-state transition logic) and FR-NGHE-010.3 (redundancy architecture)
DP-B is independent of vehicle propulsion, sensor payload, and operator interface design
DP-B couples to network infrastructure selection (carrier, technology) but does not mandate specific carriers
coupling_check.notes: DP-B is a well-decoupled solution: it addresses link availability through architectural redundancy without constraining other system functions. The coupling to safe-state logic is acceptable and expected.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Two independent communication carriers/paths are available at the worksite.
ASSUMPTION: Automatic failover logic can be implemented with latency < 100 ms.
RISK: If both paths fail simultaneously (e.g., regional outage), availability target cannot be met; mitigation requires site-level network diversity planning.
RISK: Cost of dual-path infrastructure may exceed budget; trade-off analysis required.
RISK: Synchronization of telemetry across dual paths may introduce complexity; requires careful state management.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-B is sound. Recommend decomposing into sub-DPs for (1) primary path selection and monitoring, (2) secondary path provisioning, (3) failover logic, and (4) link-state telemetry. Clarify carrier independence and cost implications in next level.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: The Teleoperation module owns the dual-path communication architecture, link-state monitoring, failover logic, and telemetry/tasking message routing. It interfaces with the Vehicle for command execution and with Safety Services for safe-state coordination.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator commands (movement, tool control, mode changes) from remote operator console; routed through primary or secondary path.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits vehicle state, sensor data, and link-state metrics to operator console and monitoring systems; aggregated from both paths for redundancy.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints from Safety Services; transmits link-loss alerts and safe-state confirmations.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Manages primary and secondary network connections; monitors link quality and initiates failover.

module_containment_scores.notes: Teleoperation is the natural owner of dual-path communication, failover logic, and link-state monitoring. All DP-B elements (redundancy, failover, monitoring) are cohesive and belong in this module. Minor diffusion: network carrier selection may involve Vehicle or external infrastructure teams.

Safety Services owns safe-state logic and constraints, but NOT the communication infrastructure. Link availability is a technical enabler, not a safety function per se. Assigning DP-B to Safety Services would scatter communication logic.

Vehicle owns propulsion, sensors, and actuators. Communication infrastructure is external to the vehicle platform. Assigning DP-B to Vehicle would couple vehicle design to network topology, violating separation of concerns.
NGHE Solution is the system-level container, but DP-B is a subsystem-level design. Assigning to NGHE Solution would bypass architectural layering and make ownership ambiguous.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the correct owner of DP-B (dual-path communication with failover). It has the highest containment score (0.95) and lowest diffusion (0.05). All sub-functions (path selection, monitoring, failover, telemetry routing) are cohesive and belong together. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and do not leak internal logic.
containment_risks: RISK: Network carrier provisioning and SLA management may require involvement of external infrastructure teams; recommend clear interface contract.
RISK: Safe-state transition logic (FR-NGHE-010.2) requires coordination with Safety Services; ensure SafetyAlert interface is bidirectional and timely.
RISK: Link-state telemetry must be accurate and low-latency; recommend dedicated monitoring thread/process within Teleoperation module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign DP-B to Teleoperation module. Clarify ownership of network carrier contracts and SLA management (likely external to NGHE). Define SafetyAlert interface protocol (link-loss detection → safe-state trigger) in detail. Specify link-state monitoring frequency and accuracy requirements.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (dual-path failover) directly addresses FR-NGHE-010 (link availability ≥ 0.999). The logic is sound: redundancy and automatic failover are proven techniques for achieving high availability. Assumptions (carrier availability, failover latency) are reasonable and testable. No fundamental mismatch between requirement and solution.

diffusion_risks: Network carrier selection and provisioning may diffuse to external teams; recommend clear SLA contract.
Safe-state logic (FR-NGHE-010.2) requires SafetyAlert interface; ensure Teleoperation and Safety Services coordinate on link-loss response.
Vehicle command execution (Tasking interface) must tolerate failover latency; recommend testing with realistic failover scenarios.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has high cohesion for DP-B. All communication logic (dual paths, failover, monitoring) is contained. External interfaces are well-defined and do not leak internal design. Ownership is clear.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Link availability can be measured directly via continuous monitoring of link state (up/down) during evaluation period. Calculation is straightforward: (total_up_time / total_evaluation_time). Teleoperation module can log link transitions for audit. Feasibility is high.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-010 is allocated to Teleoperation module. Sub-FRs (010.1 link-loss detection, 010.2 safe-state transition, 010.3 redundancy, 010.4 logging) are clear and traceable. Allocation is unambiguous.

recommended_revisions: Define link-loss detection latency requirement (FR-NGHE-010.1) explicitly; recommend ≤ 100 ms.
Define safe-state transition protocol (FR-NGHE-010.2) in SafetyAlert interface specification.
Specify network carrier independence and SLA requirements in DP-B detail design.
Plan for evaluation period duration and measurement methodology in verification plan.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-010 is not atomic; it decomposes into four sub-FRs (link-loss detection, safe-state transition, redundancy architecture, logging). DP-B is sound and well-allocated to Teleoperation module. The next level of decomposition is necessary to detail failover logic, safe-state coordination, and monitoring. Proceed with functional decomposition of sub-FRs.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-010.1: The teleoperation system shall detect link loss within 100 milliseconds.
FR-NGHE-010.2: The teleoperation system shall initiate safe-state transition (e.g., brake engagement, mode change) upon link loss, coordinated via SafetyAlert interface.
FR-NGHE-010.3: The teleoperation system shall provision and manage dual independent communication paths (primary + secondary) with automatic failover.
FR-NGHE-010.4: The teleoperation system shall log all link state transitions (up/down, failover events) with timestamp and reason for availability audit.

assumptions: Two independent communication carriers/paths are available at the worksite.
Automatic failover logic can be implemented with latency < 100 ms.
Link availability is measured as continuous uptime ratio over the agreed evaluation period.
Safe-state transition is coordinated with Safety Services via SafetyAlert interface.
Network carrier provisioning and SLA management are external to NGHE module ownership.
open_risks: Simultaneous failure of both communication paths (regional outage) cannot be mitigated by DP-B alone; requires site-level network diversity planning.
Cost of dual-path infrastructure may exceed budget; trade-off analysis required.
Synchronization of telemetry across dual paths may introduce complexity; requires careful state management.
Safe-state transition logic must be fast and reliable; inadequate implementation could compromise safety.
evidence_needed: Carrier availability and SLA data for primary and secondary paths at representative worksites.
Failover latency measurements from prototype or simulation.
Safety analysis of link-loss scenarios and safe-state transition logic.
Evaluation period duration and measurement methodology (continuous monitoring vs. sampling).
Telemetry synchronization strategy across dual paths.
